prompt: Define the 2026 shared design language of top AI infrastructure brands: anthropic.com, scale.com, together.ai, groq.com, modal.com (plus baseten, fal.ai if relevant). Across these sites, what are the SHARED moves designers identify: type systems (the serif-revival vs neo-grotesque split — which exact faces: Styrene? Tiempos? Suisse? ABC Diatype? GT America?), background materials (warm paper vs cold dark vs gradient mesh), illustration/diagram languages, border-and-hairline usage, card and code-block styling, and density/whitespace norms. Cite Fonts In Use, brand-refresh announcements, agency credits, and verbatim X commentary (@handle + date). Conclude with a concrete 'house style of AI infra 2026' spec: faces, weights, spacing, palette tendencies, materials — implementable by a dev.
content:
**Anthropic leads with a distinctive serif-revival + custom sans pairing (Styrene headlines + Tiempos body, plus proprietary Anthropic Serif), while others favor geometric/modern sans (e.g., Futura homage on Together.ai) or custom wordmarks.** Shared traits across these AI infra sites emphasize clean, developer-friendly minimalism with tech-forward restraint, humanizing touches (especially Anthropic), and functional density suited to code/docs-heavy content.[[1]](https://type.today/en/journal/anthropic)[[2]](https://fontofweb.com/tokens/anthropic.com)

**Type systems (serif-revival vs. neo-grotesque split):**  
- **Anthropic.com** prominently uses **Styrene** (Berton Hasebe for Commercial Type; a narrow, squarish neo-grotesque/sans experiment with extended details on f/j/r/t) for headlines and subheadings. **Tiempos** (Klim Type Foundry; transitional serif) for body text. They also deploy a custom proprietary **Anthropic Serif** (designed with Bspk Llc / Geist) in interfaces like Claude chats for an elegant, readable serif feel. This creates the clearest “serif revival vs. modern sans” split.[[1]](https://type.today/en/journal/anthropic)[[3]](https://deardesigner.substack.com/p/my-styrene-soul-a-short-affair-with)[[2]](https://fontofweb.com/tokens/anthropic.com)  
- **Together.ai** uses **The Future** (their primary typeface, a careful geometric homage to Futura by Paul Renner, with avant-garde alternates) for headlines and body.[[4]](https://www.together.ai/brand)  
- Others (Groq, Scale, Modal, Baseten, fal.ai) show less publicly documented specifics but lean toward restrained, custom or system sans (e.g., Groq’s Big Monocle-designed wordmark and system; fal.ai’s three-pronged typographic system evoking camera/typewriter/processor). No widespread evidence of Suisse, ABC Diatype, or GT America as primary faces; the split favors Styrene/Tiempos-style pairings or pure geometric sans over other named revivals.[[5]](https://dillonblue.com/Groq)[[6]](https://www.instagram.com/p/DARSRbqxD2p/)

**Background materials:** Anthropic favors a friendlier, less “intimidating” tech aesthetic (light/warm paper-like or clean white with subtle textures for human/AI-safety vibe). Infra-focused peers (Groq, Modal, Together, Scale, fal.ai, Baseten) default to cold dark modes, deep navy/black, or subtle gradient meshes for code/console immersion and high-contrast readability. Fal.ai stands out as bolder/more expressive.[[1]](https://type.today/en/journal/anthropic)

**Illustration/diagram languages:** Minimal and functional—diagrams emphasize computation, data flow, or chip/LPU architecture (e.g., Groq’s graphic patterns referencing information processing). Anthropic incorporates warmer, approachable photography (e.g., meals) alongside clean diagrams. Fal.ai leans generative/media-oriented visuals. Overall: restrained line work, abstract computational motifs, and high-fidelity product screenshots over ornate illustration.

**Border-and-hairline usage:** Thin, precise hairlines and subtle borders for cards/sections (common in dev sites for separation without clutter). Anthropic and Together emphasize elegance and modernity; Groq uses restrained, essential elements.

**Card and code-block styling:** Clean, elevated cards with subtle shadows or borders, generous internal padding, and monospaced code blocks (often dark-themed with syntax highlighting). High contrast, rounded or sharp corners depending on brand (softer for Anthropic, sharper for infra players). Code blocks are prominent and readable, aligning with developer focus.

**Density/whitespace norms:** Moderate-to-generous whitespace for premium, uncluttered feel—prioritizing scannability in marketing/docs over dense packing. Anthropic feels more open/human; pure infra sites balance this with functional density around code/console elements.

**Citations and sources:** Key details draw from Fonts In Use-adjacent analysis (type.today on Anthropic’s Styrene/Tiempos), brand sites (Together.ai brand page), agency credits (Big Monocle for Groq; recent fal.ai identity work), and design tokens/pages (fontofweb.com on Anthropic). Limited verbatim X commentary in recent results; broader searches show ongoing discussion of Anthropic’s evolving UI/fonts but few dated @handle critiques of the full cohort.[[1]](https://type.today/en/journal/anthropic)[[4]](https://www.together.ai/brand)[[5]](https://dillonblue.com/Groq)

**Concrete “house style of AI infra 2026” spec (implementable by a dev):**  
- **Faces:** Headlines: Styrene (or close geometric neo-grotesque like Inter/Neue Haas Grotesk variants) at 700–900 weight. Body: Tiempos or system serif fallback (Georgia) at 400–500. UI/code: Monospace (e.g., SF Mono or custom) + geometric sans primary (Futura homage or The Future if licensed). Custom serif option for trust/human touch.  
- **Weights:** 400/500 regular, 600/700 medium-bold, 800+ for emphasis.  
- **Spacing:** 1.5–1.75 line-height for body; generous margins (e.g., 2–4rem sections); 8–12px hairline borders.  
- **Palette tendencies:** Dark mode default (#0a0a0a or deep navy #0f172a bg); accents in electric cyan/blue (#00f0ff or #3b82f6) or subtle green for “AI” energy; warm off-white (#f8f7f4 paper) for Anthropic-style light sections. High contrast (WCAG AA+).  
- **Materials:** Subtle gradients or mesh for depth on dark; clean paper texture lightly on light. Thin borders, elevated cards (subtle shadow or border), monospaced code with dark bg.  
- **Overall:** Prioritize restraint, developer ergonomics, and selective warmth. Use Tailwind or similar with custom font stacks; test on code-heavy pages for density balance.[[4]](https://www.together.ai/brand)

This reflects a maturing 2026 aesthetic blending Swiss/modernist roots with AI-specific humanization and computational motifs.
